expectations.text: States a formal precondition (e.g., array is sorted, lo <= hi)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 explicitly states: 'Pre: arr is a sorted array of integers (∀i ∈ [0, len(arr)-1): arr[i] <= arr[i+1])'

expectations.text: States a formal postcondition (e.g., returns index i such that arr[i] == target, or -1)
expectations.evidence: Post specifies: 'result = -1 if target not found, result = i if arr[i] == target and lo <= i <= hi'

expectations.text: Shows derivation steps leading to the code, not just the code
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Derivación del Algoritmo' shows analysis of postcondition structure, loop invariant I, bounding function t(lo_curr, hi_curr), and step-by-step body logic for three branches (arr[mid]==target, arr[mid]>target, arr[mid]<target)

expectations.text: Derives test cases from the postcondition (e.g., target present, target absent, boundary)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 lists 11 test cases explicitly derived from postcondition branches: base case (single element), target-found-at-boundaries, target-not-found branches, range boundaries, duplicates. Each test cites the branch it covers.

expectations.text: The resulting binary search implementation is actually correct (handles all cases)
expectations.evidence: Implementation in Section 3 is correct. Section 5 provides correctness verification proving invariant maintenance, termination, and both postcondition branches. Handles lo/hi boundaries, duplicates, and edge cases.
